description: National Science Foundation SBIR Phase I Project Pitch - 4-section pre-proposal with strict character limits

technology_innovation.name: Technology Innovation
technology_innovation.guidelines: Describe the technology innovation clearly and concisely within 3,500 characters. STRUCTURE: (1) State the specific technical problem and why current solutions are inadequate — use concrete evidence, not claims; (2) Describe YOUR innovation — what is technically new, not just 'better'; (3) Explain the underlying scientific or engineering principle that makes this work; (4) State the current Technology Readiness Level (TRL) with evidence of preliminary work. CRITICAL RULES: Do NOT fabricate technical claims, performance numbers, or hypotheses — every number must come from the company context provided. Do NOT use phrases like 'we believe', 'we hope', or 'novel approach' without concrete differentiation. Do NOT exceed 3,500 characters.
technology_innovation.description: Core technical innovation: what it is, why it matters, what makes it genuinely new

technical_objectives.name: Technical Objectives and Challenges
technical_objectives.guidelines: Define 2-4 specific, measurable Phase I objectives within 3,500 characters. STRUCTURE: (1) List numbered objectives — each must have a measurable success criterion (e.g., 'achieve X metric at Y threshold'); (2) For each objective, identify the key technical challenge that makes it non-trivial; (3) Briefly describe the methodology or approach for each objective; (4) Include at least one go/no-go decision point. CRITICAL RULES: Objectives must be achievable within $275K and 6 months — do not propose full product development. Every success criterion must be quantifiable. Do NOT fabricate benchmarks, datasets, or test results. Do NOT exceed 3,500 characters.
technical_objectives.description: Specific Phase I objectives with measurable success criteria and key technical challenges

market_opportunity.name: Market Opportunity
market_opportunity.guidelines: Describe the market opportunity in 1,750 characters or fewer. STRUCTURE: (1) Define the target market using BOTTOM-UP sizing — name specific customer types and estimate how many exist, what they currently spend on the problem, and your addressable segment; (2) Identify 1-2 specific competitors or alternative approaches and explain your differentiation; (3) State your business model in one sentence (how you make money). CRITICAL RULES: Do NOT use top-down TAM/SAM/SOM from analyst reports. Do NOT claim 'no competitors exist'. Do NOT fabricate market size numbers, customer counts, or revenue projections — use only data from the company context provided. Do NOT exceed 1,750 characters.
market_opportunity.description: Bottom-up market sizing with named customer types and honest competitive positioning

company_and_team.name: Company and Team
company_and_team.guidelines: Describe the company and key personnel in 1,750 characters or fewer. STRUCTURE: (1) Company name, location, founding year, and core capability in 1-2 sentences; (2) Principal Investigator — name, highest degree, most relevant experience, and % effort commitment (must be >50%); (3) 1-2 other key team members with their specific roles and relevant qualifications; (4) Any critical partnerships or advisory relationships. CRITICAL RULES: Do NOT fabricate degrees, publications, prior grants, or experience. Use only team information from the company context provided. If team data is sparse, keep it brief rather than inventing credentials. Do NOT exceed 1,750 characters.
company_and_team.description: Company credentials and team qualifications connected to this specific project

intellectual_merit.description: The potential to advance knowledge - this is 50% of your score
sub_criteria: How important is the proposed activity to advancing knowledge?
How well qualified is the proposer to conduct the project?
To what extent does the proposed activity suggest and explore creative, original concepts?
How well conceived and organized is the proposed activity?
Is there sufficient access to resources?
What is the potential for the proposed activity to benefit society?
To what extent does the activity promote teaching, training, and learning?
How well does the activity broaden participation of underrepresented groups?
Will results be disseminated broadly to enhance scientific understanding?
May the results be used to enhance infrastructure for research and education?
What is the market opportunity for this innovation?
How strong is the commercialization plan?
Does the team have relevant commercial experience?
Are there Letters of Intent or customer validation?

format_specifications.font: Times New Roman or Computer Modern
format_specifications.references_format: N/A - Project Pitch is a structured form, not a paginated document

special_requirements.phase_i_scope: Feasibility demonstration only, not product development
special_requirements.pi_minimum_effort: Principal Investigator must dedicate >50% effort to the project

submission_requirements.format: Online form with 4 text fields (not a PDF upload)
submission_requirements.character_limits: Technology Innovation: 3500, Technical Objectives: 3500, Market Opportunity: 1750, Company and Team: 1750
submission_requirements.review_outcome: Invite to submit full Phase I proposal, or decline

fatal_flaws_to_avoid: Exceeding character limits — content is truncated or rejected by the submission system
Fabricating technical claims, performance numbers, or benchmarks not supported by actual data
Using 'we believe' or 'we hope' — shows uncertainty, not conviction
Scope creep — trying to propose full product development instead of feasibility
Generic competitive analysis — 'no direct competitors' is always a red flag
Top-down market sizing from analyst reports instead of bottom-up analysis
Missing preliminary data — some evidence of feasibility is expected
